title: The Epistemic Regress of Operator Discovery
subtitle: Foundationalism, Russell's Acquaintance, and the Grounding Problem for Learned Primitives

children.children: When we discover Schankian operators like ATRANS and PTRANS via wake-sleep compression, what justifies them? The events they compress. But what justifies our parsing of those events? The operators we used to parse them. This is circular — the exact vicious regress that foundationalism was invented to solve 2,400 years ago. This tutorial explores how epistemology's oldest problem maps onto operator discovery, and why Russell's 1912 distinction between acquaintance and description gives us a way out.

Aristotle noticed it first: if every belief needs justification, and every justification is itself a belief that needs justification, you get an infinite chain. Either the chain never ends (impossible for finite minds), it goes in a circle (cheating), or it stops somewhere (foundationalism).

Imagine asking a child 'why?' after every answer. 'Why is the sky blue?' 'Because of Rayleigh scattering.' 'Why does Rayleigh scattering work?' 'Because of the wavelength of light.' 'Why does light have wavelengths?' ... Eventually you hit bedrock — things you just know directly, not through other things. Those are your foundations.

children.definition: The problem that every justified belief seems to require justification from another belief, creating an infinite chain.
The view that some beliefs are basic — they don't need justification from other beliefs. These 'foundational' beliefs stop the regress.
The rival view: beliefs justify each other in a web, with no privileged foundations. Like a puzzle where pieces support each other.
To be justified in believing P based on evidence E, you must be (1) justified in believing E, and (2) justified in believing E makes P probable.

children.children: Coherentism says beliefs justify each other mutually — no foundations needed. But consider: a perfectly coherent fantasy novel has internally consistent beliefs ('dragons breathe fire because they have flint organs') without any being TRUE. Coherence without grounding is fiction. For operator discovery, a set of operators that explain each other but never connect to observed data is just a self-consistent hallucination.
Here's the regress applied to our Schankian operator discovery system:
Step 1: We discover ATRANS (transfer of possession) from event data.
Q: What justifies ATRANS? A: The events it compresses — 'give', 'sell', 'steal' all share this pattern.
Q: What justifies our parsing of those events? A: The operators we used to decompose them.
Q: But those operators include ATRANS! We're going in a circle.

ATRANS justifies → event parsing justifies → operator library justifies → ATRANS
    ↑                                                                         |
    └─────────────────── CIRCULAR! ───────────────────────────────────────────┘

The wake-sleep compression algorithm has exactly this structure: the SLEEP phase uses the current library to parse events, and the WAKE phase updates the library based on those parsings. It's bootstrapping — and bootstrapping IS the regress.

This isn't just a philosophical curiosity. If operator discovery is circular, it could converge to any self-consistent set of operators — not necessarily the 'right' ones. MDL (Minimum Description Length) helps constrain this, but MDL itself assumes the data representation, which assumes operators...

props.title: 3. Russell's Way Out: Acquaintance vs. Description

children.children: In 1912, Bertrand Russell proposed a distinction that breaks the regress: some things you know by acquaintance (direct awareness), and some you know only by description (through concepts and propositions). Acquaintance is foundational — it doesn't need justification because you can't be wrong about it.

children.definition: Direct, non-judgmental, non-conceptual awareness. You are acquainted with your current pain, your visual experience of red, your sense of movement. No inference needed — the experience IS the knowledge.
Mediated through concepts and propositions. 'The person who committed the Whitechapel murders' — you know ABOUT Jack the Ripper through descriptions, not through direct acquaintance.

children.children: The difference between tasting chocolate (acquaintance) and reading a chemistry paper about theobromine (description). You can know everything about chocolate's molecular composition by description, but the taste itself is only available by acquaintance. No amount of description can substitute for the direct experience.

Russell's key claim: acquaintance is INFALLIBLE. Not in the sense that your beliefs about your experiences are infallible, but in the sense that the experience itself can't fail to exist when you have it. You can't be wrong about whether you're currently in pain — the pain IS the acquaintance.

Russell's test for acquaintance mirrors Descartes: Can you conceive of being in this very state when the object doesn't exist? If yes, it's not acquaintance. You can hallucinate a table (so tables aren't acquaintance-objects), but you can't hallucinate your experience of hallucinating. The experience is always real, even when its content is false.
Here's the key move: some Schankian operators are acquaintance-knowledge, and some are description-knowledge. This creates a natural foundation that stops the regress.

ACQUAINTANCE-OPERATORS (foundational, stop the regress):
  PTRANS — An embodied agent has DIRECTLY moved things.
           You know PTRANS by doing it, not by reading about it.
  GRASP  — You've directly experienced grasping.
  PROPEL — You've directly experienced pushing/pulling.
  INGEST — You've directly experienced eating/drinking.

DESCRIPTION-OPERATORS (inferential, need justification):
  ATRANS — Transfer of possession. You never directly SEE
           ownership move. It's inferred from social convention.
  MTRANS — Information transfer in another mind. You NEVER
           directly experience what happens inside someone
           else's head. Pure description.
  MBUILD — Mental construction. Abstract, never directly
           observed even in yourself (you see the result,
           not the process).

The regress stops at acquaintance-operators: PTRANS doesn't need justification because an embodied agent directly experiences movement. ATRANS, in contrast, is built FROM acquaintance-operators plus social convention (PTRANS of an object + social agreement that ownership transferred).

This maps perfectly onto the symbol grounding problem (Harnad 1990). Grounded operators = foundational beliefs (tied to sensorimotor experience). Ungrounded operators = inferential beliefs (requiring justification chains back to grounded ones). The Chinese Room thought experiment is an agent with ONLY description-knowledge and ZERO acquaintance-knowledge of its operators.

props.title: Operator Grounding Classifier
props.description: Classify each operator as acquaintance (directly experienced) or description (inferred through concepts):

items.label: PTRANS (physical transfer of location)
items.explanation: An embodied agent directly experiences moving things. The movement IS the knowledge.

items.label: ATRANS (transfer of possession/ownership)
items.explanation: Ownership is a social construct — you never directly perceive it. You infer it from observable PTRANS + social convention.

items.label: INGEST (eating, drinking, breathing)
items.explanation: Direct sensory experience. You know INGEST by doing it — taste, swallowing, breathing are all directly experienced.

items.label: MTRANS (transfer of information between minds)
items.explanation: You never directly experience what happens in someone else's mind. MTRANS is always inferred from behavior.

items.label: PROPEL (applying force to an object)
items.explanation: Pushing, pulling, throwing — all directly experienced through proprioception and muscle feedback.

items.label: MBUILD (constructing new information mentally)
items.explanation: Even introspection on your own thinking is mediated by concepts. You see the RESULT of MBUILD, not the process itself.

props.title: 5. PIJ as a Constraint on Operator Composition

children.children: The Principle of Inferential Justification (PIJ) from foundationalist epistemology gives us a formal constraint on composing operators:
To compose ATRANS ∘ PTRANS into 'mail' (sending a letter), PIJ requires:

PIJ Clause 1: You must be justified in believing ATRANS and PTRANS exist.
  → Each component operator must have sufficient evidence.
  → PTRANS is grounded (acquaintance) ✓
  → ATRANS needs evidence from multiple events ⚠️

PIJ Clause 2: You must be justified in believing they compose meaningfully.
  → Structural similarity, not just co-occurrence!
  → 'mail' events share operator + entity + role structure ✓
  → Random co-occurrence of ATRANS and PTRANS keywords ✗

Clause 2 is particularly important: it says you can't just notice that 'ATRANS' and 'PTRANS' appear together in text. You need structural evidence that they compose — shared role-filler bindings, compatible entity types, consistent temporal ordering.

PIJ Clause 2 is exactly our anti-Gettier guard! Surface keyword co-occurrence without structural isomorphism is like the palm-reading example from the SEP article: there happens to be a correlation between palm lines and lifespan (true!), but believing it based on palm-reading is unjustified because there's no structural connection. Similarly, two operators sharing keywords without sharing structure is a Gettier case.

In 1963, Edmund Gettier showed that Justified True Belief (JTB) isn't sufficient for knowledge. You can have a belief that's true AND justified, yet isn't genuine knowledge because the justification is accidentally connected to the truth.

props.title: A Gettier Case for Operator Discovery

children.children: Suppose our system discovers that events about 'manifold' and 'fiber' converge across the analogical and conjectural worlds. It boosts conviction and promotes the connection to the actual world.

But the analogical event was about fiber optic cables (physical infrastructure), while the conjectural event was about fiber bundles (mathematical topology). They share keywords but describe completely different things.

The promoted 'fact' happens to be true (there IS a connection between fiber optics and fiber bundles — both involve parallel transport!), but the justification was accidental. This is a Gettier case: justified, true, but not genuine knowledge.

Surface match:   'fiber' + 'manifold' appear in both events     ✓
Structural match: same operator? same entities? same roles?     ✗
Gettier verdict:  TRUE belief, JUSTIFIED belief, NOT knowledge  !

Our anti-Gettier guard catches this: it requires structural similarity (shared operators, entities, and role-filler patterns) above a threshold of 0.3, not just keyword overlap. In practice, this blocked 30 false convergences in our first real run.

props.title: Gettier Detector
props.description: For each pair of events, decide: genuine convergence or Gettier case?

items.question:
Event A (conjectural): 'ATRANS(agent=hydra, object=knowledge, source=phrase-structure, dest=thematic-role)'
Event B (abductive): 'ATRANS(agent=hydra, object=features, source=head-1, dest=head-2)'
options: Genuine convergence
Gettier case
items.explanation: Same operator (ATRANS), compatible entities (both about Hydra's information transfer), compatible roles. Structural similarity is high.

items.question:
Event A (analogical): 'Manifold origami folds linguistic space'
Event B (conjectural): 'Garden paths are manifold misidentifications'
options: Genuine convergence
Gettier case
items.explanation: They share the keyword 'manifold' but use different operators, different entities, and incompatible role structures. The connection is real but the keyword match is accidental — a Gettier case.

items.question:
Event A (abductive): 'Inference direction: must → abductive (Cumming & Winans)'
Event B (conjectural): 'Abductive conviction updates are slower (0.7× multiplier)'
options: Genuine convergence
Gettier case
items.explanation: Both use compatible operators about the same theoretical construct (abductive inference), with matching entity references. Structural similarity is high (0.85).

props.title: 7. The Symbol Grounding Problem IS the Regress

children.children: Harnad's symbol grounding problem (1990) asks: how do symbols get their meaning? If you define 'horse' as 'a large four-legged animal' and 'animal' as 'a living organism that moves' and 'organism' as... you're in a regress. Definitions define other definitions.

This is EXACTLY the epistemic regress applied to meaning instead of belief. And the solution is the same: some symbols must be grounded directly in experience (acquaintance), not through other symbols (description).

Imagine a dictionary written in a language you don't speak. Every word is defined using other words in the same unknown language. No matter how many definitions you read, you'll never understand any of them — you need at least SOME words to be grounded in something outside the dictionary. Those grounded words are your 'acquaintance-symbols.' For operators: PTRANS, GRASP, and INGEST are grounded in embodied experience. ATRANS and MTRANS are defined through them.

Searle's Chinese Room (1980) is an agent with ONLY description-knowledge. It manipulates symbols according to rules but has zero acquaintance with what those symbols mean. In our framework: it has operators (rules for symbol manipulation) but none of them are grounded. Every operator is justified only by other operators — the regress never stops. This is why the Chinese Room 'doesn't understand Chinese' — it lacks foundational acquaintance-operators.

LLMs may be in a similar position. They have description-knowledge of PTRANS (they can use the word 'move' correctly) without acquaintance-knowledge (they've never moved anything). Our prediction: operators discovered from embodied models (VLAs) should converge faster and more robustly than text-only operators, because they have genuine foundations.
Putting it all together, every discovered operator can be characterized along four independent dimensions:

children.definition: What world does this knowledge live in? Actual (verified), conjectural (hypothesized), abductive (inferred from effects), analogical (mapped from another domain).
Logical (proved by deduction), semantic (accepted by convention), systemic (axiomatic), empirical (accumulated through observation via the illative sense).

children.term: Knowledge Type (Krathwohl/Bloom) — What kind of thing
children.definition: Factual (discrete facts), conceptual (relationships and categories), procedural (how-to knowledge), metacognitive (knowledge about knowledge itself).

children.term: Epistemic Access (Russell 1912) — How you access it
children.definition: Acquaintance (direct, non-judgmental, foundational — stops the regress) vs. description (mediated through concepts, inferential — requires justification chain).

children.children:
PTRANS (physical transfer of location):
  Modality:         actual (grounded in physical reality)
  Warrant:          empirical (observed through embodied experience)
  Knowledge type:   procedural (you know HOW to move things)
  Epistemic access: ACQUAINTANCE (directly experienced)
  
  → Conviction: 0.95 immediately. No regress. Foundational.

ATRANS (transfer of possession):
  Modality:         conjectural (inferred social convention)
  Warrant:          semantic (depends on social agreement)
  Knowledge type:   conceptual (understanding of ownership)
  Epistemic access: DESCRIPTION (never directly observed)
  
  → Conviction: starts at 0.5, requires convergent evidence
     from multiple sources + structural similarity to promote.

props.title: Four-Dimensional Operator Classifier
props.description: The four dimensions create a rich space for characterizing discovered operators. Each combination predicts different conviction dynamics:

children.children: This framework makes concrete predictions that distinguish it from 'just another classification scheme':

children.definition: Operators discovered from VLA (Vision-Language-Action) models, where PTRANS/GRASP have physical grounding, should reach high conviction in fewer training steps than the same operators discovered from text-only corpora.
PTRANS and INGEST should appear in every language's operator library because they're grounded in universal embodied experience. ATRANS may vary across cultures with different property conventions.

children.term: Prediction 3: Gettier rate predicts library quality
children.definition: An operator library with more Gettier cases (surface convergences without structural support) should have worse downstream task performance than one with genuine structural convergences.

children.term: Prediction 4: LLMs have description-knowledge of all operators
children.definition: LLMs should represent PTRANS and ATRANS identically in their internal representations (both are description-knowledge from text). Embodied models should show a clear acquaintance/description split.

children.question: What is the epistemic regress problem?
options: The problem that AI models need more data over time
The problem that every justified belief seems to require justification from another belief, creating an infinite chain
The problem that neural networks forget old information when learning new things
The problem that operators can't be composed without losing information
children.explanation: The epistemic regress is the observation that if every belief needs justification, and justifications are themselves beliefs, you get an infinite chain — or need foundations.

children.question: In Russell's framework, knowledge by acquaintance is:
options: Knowledge gained through reading books
Knowledge shared between friends
Direct, non-judgmental awareness that doesn't require inference
Knowledge that has been verified by multiple sources
children.explanation: Acquaintance is direct awareness — experiencing pain, seeing red, feeling movement. It's non-judgmental (not a belief about the experience) and non-inferential (no reasoning required).

children.question: Why is ATRANS (transfer of possession) description-knowledge rather than acquaintance?
options: Because it involves abstract objects
Because you never directly perceive ownership changing — it's inferred from physical actions plus social convention
Because it's a compound operator
Because Schank classified it that way originally
children.explanation: Ownership is invisible. When you 'give' someone a book, what you directly experience is PTRANS (moving the book). The transfer of ownership is a social interpretation layered on top — pure description.

children.question: What does the anti-Gettier guard require for convergence to count as genuine?
options: That the events share at least 5 keywords
That the events come from the same source
Structural similarity (shared operators, entities, roles) above a threshold, not just keyword overlap
That both events have high conviction already
children.explanation: Surface keyword overlap can be accidental (Gettier cases). The guard requires structural isomorphism — same operators, compatible entities, matching role-fillers — to count as genuine convergent evidence.

children.question: How does the symbol grounding problem relate to the epistemic regress?
options: They're unrelated problems from different fields
They're the same problem applied to different domains: the regress is about belief justification, grounding is about meaning justification — both need foundations
Symbol grounding is a solution to the regress
The regress only applies to symbolic AI, not neural networks
children.explanation: Both are the same infinite-chain problem: beliefs justified by beliefs (regress) ↔ symbols defined by symbols (grounding). Both are solved by foundations: basic beliefs (foundationalism) ↔ grounded symbols (embodied experience).

children.term: SEP: Foundationalist Theories of Epistemic Justification
children.definition: https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/justep-foundational/ — The authoritative reference on PIJ and the regress argument.

children.term: SEP: Knowledge by Acquaintance vs. Description

children.definition: https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/knowledge-analysis/ — The Gettier problem and its implications.
The original paper connecting meaning to embodied experience.

children.term: Roadmap for Embodied Grounding in LLMs (arXiv:2409.16900)
children.definition: https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.16900 — Modern perspective on grounding language models in action.
